metadata.seed: Stu and I were talking about it before I came in, and I can't really figure out why I'm so bothered. We have such a great relationship. I mean, I feel lucky as a woman in the 21st century to have a man who cares about her and takes her out and makes her feel appreciative. I've never had that before. And I go home happy. I go home excited to see him. I can't name a single friend or even my sisters who have that. And still all I hear is there are criticisms, there are words, how weird it is, and why would a 58 year old man want to be with you? You're not even 30 yet, you know? And I just don't see that. I don't see age being an issue. But again, like, I'm just, I'm waking up with those thoughts.

patient.query: Stu and I were talking about it before I came in, and I can't really figure out why I'm so bothered. We have such a great relationship. I mean, I feel lucky as a woman in the 21st century to have a man who cares about her and takes her out and makes her feel appreciative. I've never had that before. And I go home happy. I go home excited to see him. I can't name a single friend or even my sisters who have that. And still all I hear is there are criticisms, there are words, how weird it is, and why would a 58 year old man want to be with you? You're not even 30 yet, you know? And I just don't see that. I don't see age being an issue. But again, like, I'm just, I'm waking up with those thoughts.

patient.query: I guess there's a part of me that just takes those criticisms to heart, even if they come from friends or family who might not know everything. Sometimes I notice little things that bother me, like my partner not being as supportive as I'd hope. But I also wonder if I'm just being too sensitive or overthinking it. It's frustrating.

patient.query: I think I’d probably tell myself it’s okay to feel what I’m feeling, that it’s valid to question things. But at the same time, I might also say not to dwell on it too much and to focus on the good moments we have. It’s hard to find that balance, though. I want to be realistic but don’t want to ruin what feels good.

It’s like I don’t want to spoil the good times, but I also don’t want to keep everything bottled up. It's tough.

patient.query: I suppose a lot of it comes from past experiences where I’ve tried to express myself and it didn’t go well. People didn’t always react the way I hoped, so that makes me hesitant. But, I guess there have been times when people did listen and we worked through stuff together. It’s just hard to remember those moments when I’m feeling anxious. It’s like my brain goes straight to the worst-case scenario.
